title: Create True-Shape Links
text:
2 Load Data

Roadway Centerlines
Same source, layer, where= filter, and whitespace-stripping as 01_node_classification.qmd to ensure the merged geometry is built from the identical feature set used for snapping.


Show the code
gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines'].as_posix()}"

COUNTY_FIPS = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
county_filter = "(" + ", ".join(f"'{f}'" for f in COUNTY_FIPS) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    gdb_path,
    layer="RCLWasatchFrontAll",
    where=(
        f"(DOT_FCLASS IN ('Interstate', 'Local', 'Major Collector', "
        f"'Minor Arterial', 'Minor Collector', 'Other Freeway', 'Principal Arterial') "
        f"OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%C%') "
        f"AND CARTOCODE <> '99' "
        f"AND (COUNTY_L IN {county_filter} OR COUNTY_R IN {county_filter})"
    ),
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Centerlines: {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")


Centerlines: 25,358 features | CRS: EPSG:26912




Snapped Nodes


Show the code
gdf_nodes_snapped = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_node_snapped"], layer="nodes_snapped").to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_cc_snapped    = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_node_snapped"], layer="cc_nodes_snapped").to_crs(CRS_UTM)

n_total   = len(gdf_nodes_snapped)
n_snapped = gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"].fillna(False).sum()
print(f"Nodes: {n_total:,} total | {n_snapped:,} snapped ({n_snapped/n_total:.1%})")
print(f"CC attachment nodes: {len(gdf_cc_snapped):,}")


Nodes: 21,921 total | 3,826 snapped (17.5%)
CC attachment nodes: 6,531




Show the code
gdf_nodes_snapped[gdf_nodes_snapped["snapped"]].explore()

title: Create True-Shape Links
text:
3 Part A — Resolve Snap Coordinates
nodes_snapped.gpkg stores snapped_x_round / snapped_y_round rounded to 2 decimal places (cm precision). Splitting and M-value operations require coordinates that exactly match a vertex stored in the centerline geometry. For each snapped node, this step finds the nearest unique centerline vertex within 5 cm and records its full-precision coordinate.
FixedTransit_Rail nodes are excluded — they snap to GTFS rail infrastructure (CRT stations, LRT midpoints), not to road centerlines, so they are never road centerline split points.


Show the code
gdf_nodes_road = gdf_nodes_snapped[
    gdf_nodes_snapped["snap_rule"] != "FixedTransit_Rail"
]
gdf_nodes_resolved = resolve_snap_coords(
    gdf_nodes_road,
    gdf_centerlines,
    tolerance=0.05,
)

n_road_snapped = gdf_nodes_road["snapped"].fillna(False).sum()
n_resolved     = gdf_nodes_resolved["snap_resolved"].sum()
n_unresolved   = n_road_snapped - n_resolved
print(f"Coordinate resolution: {n_resolved:,} resolved | {n_unresolved:,} unresolved")


Coordinate resolution: 3,757 resolved | 0 unresolved




Show the code
if n_unresolved > 0:
    unresolved = gdf_nodes_resolved[
        gdf_nodes_resolved["snapped"].fillna(False) & ~gdf_nodes_resolved["snap_resolved"]
    ]
    print("Unresolved node sample (check snap quality):")
    print(unresolved[["N", "snap_rule", "snapped_x_round", "snapped_y_round"]].head(10).to_string())


Build the list of exact-coordinate Points used as split locations.


Show the code
resolved_mask = gdf_nodes_resolved["snap_resolved"]
snap_points_exact = [
    Point(row["x_exact"], row["y_exact"])
    for _, row in gdf_nodes_resolved[resolved_mask].iterrows()
]
print(f"Split points prepared: {len(snap_points_exact):,}")


Split points prepared: 3,757

title: Create True-Shape Links
text:
4 Part B — Merge Centerlines
Fuse consecutive segments at degree-2 vertices via linemerge. Real intersections (3+ roads meeting) remain as line endpoints and are not merged.


Show the code
print("Merging centerlines …")
cl_merged = merge_centerlines(gdf_centerlines)

if cl_merged.geom_type == "MultiLineString":
    n_components = len(cl_merged.geoms)
elif cl_merged.geom_type == "LineString":
    n_components = 1
else:
    n_components = sum(1 for g in cl_merged.geoms if g.geom_type == "LineString")

total_length_km = sum(
    g.length for g in (cl_merged.geoms if hasattr(cl_merged, "geoms") else [cl_merged])
) / 1000
print(f"Merged: {n_components:,} components | total length: {total_length_km:,.1f} km")


Merging centerlines …
Merged: 6,917 components | total length: 5,945.9 km

title: Create True-Shape Links
section: 5 Part C — Split at Snapped Nodes
text:
5 Part C — Split at Snapped Nodes
Split the merged geometry at every model-node snap point and every CC attachment snap point. Points that project to within 0.1 mm of a line’s existing endpoint are skipped (already a boundary). After all splits, each resulting piece is bounded exclusively by: - a snapped model node position, - a snapped CC attachment node position, or - a physical road intersection preserved by linemerge.


Show the code
cc_resolved = gdf_cc_snapped[gdf_cc_snapped["snap_resolved"]]
cc_snap_points = [
    Point(row["x_snap"], row["y_snap"])
    for _, row in cc_resolved.iterrows()
]
all_snap_points = snap_points_exact + cc_snap_points
n_cc_unresolved = len(gdf_cc_snapped) - len(cc_resolved)
print(
    f"Split points: {len(snap_points_exact):,} (model nodes) + "
    f"{len(cc_snap_points):,} (CC attachment, {n_cc_unresolved:,} unresolved excluded) "
    f"= {len(all_snap_points):,} total"
)


Split points: 3,757 (model nodes) + 5,935 (CC attachment, 596 unresolved excluded) = 9,692 total




Show the code
print(f"Splitting {n_components:,} line components at {len(all_snap_points):,} points …")
pieces = split_at_nodes(cl_merged, all_snap_points)
print(f"Pieces after split: {len(pieces):,}")


Splitting 6,917 line components at 9,692 points …
Pieces after split: 12,030

title: Create True-Shape Links
section: 6 Part D — Build Pieces Layer and Export
text:
6 Part D — Build Pieces Layer and Export
Extract start/end endpoint coordinates for each piece (rounded to 2 dp for matching against snapped_x_round / snapped_y_round in the next stage), then export as link_pieces.gpkg.


Show the code
start_coords = np.array([p.coords[0] for p in pieces])
end_coords   = np.array([p.coords[-1] for p in pieces])

gdf_pieces = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "piece_id": np.arange(len(pieces)),
        "x_start":  np.round(start_coords[:, 0], 2),
        "y_start":  np.round(start_coords[:, 1], 2),
        "x_end":    np.round(end_coords[:, 0], 2),
        "y_end":    np.round(end_coords[:, 1], 2),
        "length_m": np.array([p.length for p in pieces]),
        "geometry": pieces,
    },
    crs=CRS_UTM,
)

PATHS["out_link_pieces"].parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
gdf_pieces.to_file(PATHS["out_link_pieces"], driver="GPKG")
print(f"Exported {len(gdf_pieces):,} pieces → {PATHS['out_link_pieces']}")


Exported 12,030 pieces → _data\processed\link_pieces.gpkg

Show the code

print("Piece length distribution (m):")
print(f"  Min:    {lengths.min():.1f}")

print(f"  P90:    {lengths.quantile(0.90):.1f}")

print(f"Input:  {len(gdf_centerlines):,} centerline segments")
print(f"        {n_road_snapped:,} snapped road nodes ({n_resolved:,} coordinate-resolved)")
print(f"        {len(gdf_cc_snapped):,} CC attachment nodes snapped")

print(f"        {len(gdf_cc_snapped):,} CC attachment nodes → {PATHS['in_node_snapped'].name} (layer: cc_nodes_snapped)")

  Min:    1.8

Input:  25,358 centerline segments

Output: 12,030 pieces → link_pieces.gpkg

text:
2 Load Data

Roadway Centerlines


Show the code
# 1. Prepare the Path
# We use .as_posix() to handle slashes correctly regardless of OS
# gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}"
gdb_path = f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines'].as_posix()}"

# 2. View the actual layers within the gdb
gpd.list_layers(gdb_path)









name
geometry_type




0
RCL2TDMGeometry
MultiLineString

RCLWasatchFrontAll
MultiLineString









Show the code
COUNTY_FIPS = ["49003", "49057", "49011", "49035", "49049"]
county_filter = "(" + ", ".join(f"'{f}'" for f in COUNTY_FIPS) + ")"

gdf_centerlines = gpd.read_file(
    gdb_path,  # f"/vsizip/{PATHS['in_centerlines']}",
    layer="RCLWasatchFrontAll",
    where=f"(DOT_FCLASS IN ('Interstate', 'Local', 'Major Collector', 'Minor Arterial', 'Minor Collector', 'Other Freeway', 'Principal Arterial') OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' OR DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%C%') AND CARTOCODE <> '99' AND (COUNTY_L IN {county_filter} OR COUNTY_R IN {county_filter})",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

# Strip trailing whitespace from categorical fields (source-data artefact)
for col in ["DOT_FCLASS", "CARTOCODE", "STATUS", "ONEWAY", "VERT_LEVEL", "POSTTYPE"]:
    if col in gdf_centerlines.columns:
        gdf_centerlines[col] = gdf_centerlines[col].str.strip()

print(f"Centerlines: {len(gdf_centerlines):,} features | CRS: {gdf_centerlines.crs}")


Centerlines: 25,358 features | CRS: EPSG:26912




Show the code
gdf_centerlines.head(1000).explore()




Network Links and Nodes


Show the code
# Active 2027 links, excluding HOV / Managed Lane / Managed Lane Access (FT 37-39).
# These are filtered at load time so they never inflate LinkCount, contribute to
# the direction cascade, or generate spurious "surface" node classifications.
gdf_links = gpd.read_file(
    PATHS["in_network_links"],
    where="LN_2027 > 0 AND FT_2027 NOT IN (1, 37, 38, 39)",
).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_nodes = gpd.read_file(PATHS["in_network_nodes"]).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

Nodes: 21,921




Show the code
gdf_links.head(1000).explore()
gdf_nodes.head(1000).explore()




GTFS Rail Stops
Filter GTFS stops to Light Rail (TRAX, route_type 0) and Commuter Rail (FrontRunner, route_type 2) only, walking the routes → trips → stop_times chain.


Show the code
feed = gk.read_feed(PATHS["in_gtfs"], dist_units="km")

gdf_stops  = gk.get_stops(feed,  as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_shapes = gk.get_shapes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)
gdf_routes = gk.get_routes(feed, as_gdf=True).to_crs(CRS_UTM)

RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES = {0, 2}   # 0 = Light Rail (TRAX), 2 = Commuter Rail (FrontRunner)

rail_route_ids = feed.routes.loc[feed.routes["route_type"].isin(RAIL_ROUTE_TYPES), "route_id"]
rail_trip_ids  = feed.trips.loc[feed.trips["route_id"].isin(rail_route_ids), "trip_id"]
rail_stop_ids  = feed.stop_times.loc[feed.stop_times["trip_id"].isin(rail_trip_ids), "stop_id"].unique()

gdf_rail_stops = gdf_stops[gdf_stops["stop_id"].isin(rail_stop_ids)].copy()
print(f"Rail stops: {len(gdf_rail_stops):,} of {len(gdf_stops):,} total stops")


Rail stops: 130 of 5,499 total stops




Show the code
gdf_rail_stops.explore()

title: Node Classification
text:
3 Part A — Origin Processing (Network Nodes)

A1 — Functional Type Flags
nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, ft_codes) returns True for any node whose N appears as endpoint A or B of at least one link whose FT code is in ft_codes.
Flags are non-exclusive — a node touching both a freeway and a ramp link will be True for both Freeway and Ramp. Mutual exclusion is enforced later in the topology cascade (§A3).


Show the code
# ── Three mutually exclusive TDM link classes (2027 network year) ─────────
#
# FT_2027 code reference
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Class 1 — Freeway Mainline (FW_CODES):
#   12-15  Expressway (limited-access, divided)
#   22-26  MM freeway (posted 55-75 mph, MM series)
#   32-36  Freeway    (posted 55-75 mph, Fwy series)
#   40     Express / managed-access freeway
#   FT=2 AND DIRECTION=1: directional Principal Arterial links representing
#         physically separated roadways (e.g. divided expressways coded as PA).
#
# Class 2 — Ramps and CDs (RAMP_CODES):
#   20/30  Loop ramps  |  28/29  MM on/off ramps  |  41/42  Fwy on/off ramps
#   21/31  CD roads (collector-distributor geometry)
#
# Class 3 — Surface Streets (SURFACE_CODES):
#   2/3  Principal/Minor Arterial  |  4/5  Major/Minor Collector  |  6/7  Local/Unpaved
#   FT=2 links with DIRECTION=1 are excluded (they belong to Class 1 above).
#
# Filtered out at load time (not present in gdf_links):
#   1  Centroid connector (zone-to-network tap, not a physical road)
#   37 HOV lane  |  38 Managed lane  |  39 Managed-lane access point
#   Removed before any processing so they cannot inflate LinkCount/NeighborCount,
#   corrupt the pseudonode LinkClassCount check, pollute the direction cascade,
#   or produce spurious surface-type node classifications.
#
# Loaded but excluded from the three road classes:
#   70/80 Fixed transit  |  N ranges 10000-19999 and 50000-59999 (station nodes)
#   Transit geometry is handled separately in the FixedTransit snapping pass.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

FT_COL = "FT_2027"

# DIRECTION=1 flag: handles both numeric 1 and string "1" encodings
_oneway = gdf_links["ONEWAY"].astype(str).str.strip() == "1"

# Mutually exclusive link masks (LN_2027 > 0 already filtered at load time)

# 1. Freeway includes interstates and expressways/principal arterials that have directional travel
FWY_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40}) | (
    gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({2, 12, 13, 14, 15}) & _oneway
)

# 2. Ramp and Collector-Distributor Roads share the same mask
RAMP_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({20, 21, 28, 29, 30, 31, 41, 42})

# 3. Centroid Connectors (FT = 1)
CC_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL] == 1

# 4. HOV and Managed Lanes (FT = 37, 38, 39)
MANAGED_LINK_MASK = gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({37, 38, 39})

# 5. Remaining Links get assigned as Surface Links
SURFACE_LINK_MASK = ~(FWY_LINK_MASK | RAMP_LINK_MASK | CC_LINK_MASK | MANAGED_LINK_MASK)

# FW_CODES: FT codes used for the freeway-priority direction cascade.
# FT=2+DIRECTION=1 is classified as freeway but excluded here because its
# DIRECTION value is a one-way flag, not a cardinal (NB/SB/EB/WB).
# Direction matching for those links is deferred to the geometric heading
# approach planned for the ramp refactor (Request 3).
FW_CODES = {12, 13, 14, 15, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36, 40}

gdf_nodes["Freeway"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[FWY_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["Ramp"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[RAMP_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["Surface"] = nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links[SURFACE_LINK_MASK])
gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] = (
    nodes_on(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, {70, 80})
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(10_000, 19_999)  # TRAX station nodes
    | gdf_nodes["N"].between(50_000, 59_999)  # FrontRunner station nodes
)

flag_cols = ["Freeway", "Ramp", "Surface", "FixedTransit"]
print("Flag counts:")
print(gdf_nodes[flag_cols].sum().rename("NodeCount").to_frame().to_string())
print(
    f"\nLinks by class — Freeway: {FWY_LINK_MASK.sum():,}  "
    f"| Ramp: {RAMP_LINK_MASK.sum():,}  "
    f"| Surface: {SURFACE_LINK_MASK.sum():,}"
)


Flag counts:
              NodeCount
Freeway            1885
Ramp               1634
Surface           10756
FixedTransit        610

Links by class — Freeway: 1,870  | Ramp: 1,344  | Surface: 24,779




A2 — Link Count, Neighbor Count, and Direction
count_links adds LinkCount — raw endpoint references per node, used for the distribution chart and diagnostics.
count_neighbors adds NeighborCount — the number of distinct neighboring nodes. This is the topologically correct pseudonode test: LinkCount breaks on bidirectional roads stored as two directional records (a pseudonode appears 4 times → LinkCount=4, not 2), while NeighborCount is always 2 for a true shape vertex regardless of storage convention.
A node is an is_pseudo shape vertex if and only if both conditions hold: 1. NeighborCount == 2 — lies on a single path between two real junctions. 2. LinkClassCount == 1 — all connected links share the same functional class. A node where a freeway meets a surface street has NeighborCount=2 but LinkClassCount=2, so it is a real class-transition junction and must be snapped.
assign_node_directions produces two direction columns (see docstring): - link_directions — freeway-priority cascade; used in passes (a) and (c). - fw_directions — freeway links only, majority-vote; used in pass (b) gore nodes.


Show the code
gdf_nodes = count_links(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = count_neighbors(gdf_nodes, gdf_links)
gdf_nodes = assign_node_directions(gdf_nodes, gdf_links, freeway_ft_codes=FW_CODES)

# Assign one class label per link using the already-defined mutually exclusive masks.
_link_class = pd.Series("other", index=gdf_links.index)
_link_class[FWY_LINK_MASK]  = "freeway"
_link_class[RAMP_LINK_MASK] = "ramp"
_link_class[SURFACE_LINK_MASK] = "surface"
_link_class[gdf_links[FT_COL].isin({70, 80})] = "transit"

# For each node, count how many distinct link classes touch it.
_stacked_class = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B"]].assign(cls=_link_class.values).rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
_class_counts = _stacked_class.groupby("N")["cls"].nunique()
gdf_nodes["LinkClassCount"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(_class_counts).fillna(0).astype(int)

# Count distinct FT codes per node.
# Stricter than LinkClassCount: nodes where FT changes (e.g. FT=32→33) register
# LinkFTCount=2 even though both belong to the freeway class.  Used in the future
# link true-shape dissolution step to preserve model link boundaries where posted
# speed or facility sub-type changes — dissolving those nodes would corrupt TDM impedance.
_stacked_ft = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A", FT_COL]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B", FT_COL]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
_ft_counts = _stacked_ft.groupby("N")[FT_COL].nunique()
gdf_nodes["LinkFTCount"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(_ft_counts).fillna(0).astype(int)

# Pseudonode: shape vertex on a single homogeneous path — no physical snap target.
# Uses LinkFTCount (not LinkClassCount): UGRC/UDOT centerlines are segmented at
# posted speed limit changes, so a node where FT changes (e.g. FT=32→33) has a
# real physical endpoint in the centerline data and must be sent to snapping.
# LinkClassCount would incorrectly skip those nodes (both FT codes map to "freeway"
# class), causing missed snaps at speed-transition points along freeway mainlines.
gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"] = (gdf_nodes["NeighborCount"] == 2) & (gdf_nodes["LinkFTCount"] == 1)

print(f"Pseudonodes (NeighborCount==2, single class): {gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'].sum():,}")
print(f"  of which LinkCount==2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] == 2)).sum():,}")
print(f"  of which LinkCount> 2: {(gdf_nodes['is_pseudo'] & (gdf_nodes['LinkCount'] > 2)).sum():,}  ← bidirectional road pseudonodes missed by old filter")
print(f"Class-transition nodes (NeighborCount==2, multi-class): {((gdf_nodes['NeighborCount'] == 2) & (gdf_nodes['LinkClassCount'] > 1)).sum():,}  ← real junctions, must be snapped")
print(f"FT-transition nodes now sent to snapping (NeighborCount==2, same class, diff FT): {((gdf_nodes['NeighborCount'] == 2) & (gdf_nodes['LinkClassCount'] == 1) & (gdf_nodes['LinkFTCount'] > 1)).sum():,}")

  of which LinkCount> 2: 7,459  ← bidirectional road pseudonodes missed by old filter
Class-transition nodes (NeighborCount==2, multi-class): 22  ← real junctions, must be snapped
FT-transition nodes now sent to snapping (NeighborCount==2, same class, diff FT): 127

# Link-count distribution chart
link_count_freq = gdf_nodes["LinkCount"].value_counts().sort_index()
max_val = link_count_freq.max()

for spine in ["top", "right", "left"]:
    ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_color(GRID_COLOR)

    loc="upper right", fontsize=9, frameon=False, labelcolor="#888780",

         fontsize=10.5, color="#888780", transform=ax.transAxes)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()











A3 — Node Topology Classification
Eight mutually-exclusive node_type labels. Transit is evaluated first and takes priority over all road-class combinations. The remaining seven labels derive from the full combinatorics of the (Freeway, Ramp, Surface) flags.
HOV/managed-lane nodes (FT 37-39) were filtered at load time, so they contribute no flags; those nodes fall to “surface” and are not snapped. Pseudonodes (LinkCount == 2) are shape vertices with no physical counterpart.


Show the code
no_pseudo = ~gdf_nodes["is_pseudo"]

is_fwy_node     = gdf_nodes["Freeway"]      & no_pseudo
is_ramp_node    = gdf_nodes["Ramp"]         & no_pseudo
is_surface_node = gdf_nodes["Surface"]      & no_pseudo
is_transit_node = gdf_nodes["FixedTransit"] & no_pseudo

# Full combinatorics of the three link classes → 7 node types.
# Transit is evaluated first — station nodes get "transit" regardless of any
# road-class links they may also touch.
# Most-specific road combinations (all three flags) are evaluated next.
gdf_nodes["node_type"] = np.select(
    [
        is_transit_node,                                  # fixed rail station
        is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # fwy+ramp+sf → at-grade interchange
        is_fwy_node &  is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # fwy+ramp    → elevated gore
        is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # fwy+sf      → at-grade fwy crossing
        is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # fwy only    → pure mainline
        ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node,  # ramp+sf     → ramp terminal
        ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node,  # ramp only   → mid-ramp
    ],
    ["transit", "gore_sf", "gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Node type distribution:")
print(gdf_nodes["node_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())

title: Node Classification
text:
4 Part B — Target Processing (Centerline Endpoints)
The endpoint layer makes topology and direction assignments explicit and inspectable in QGIS before any snapping happens. The full workflow is: B1 filter to active segments → B2 classify segments → B3 extract endpoints → B4 deduplicate → B5 assign directions → B6 assign ep_type → B7 export.

B1 — Filter to Active Segments


Show the code
# Exclude retired / planned segments and segments that fall outside the three
# mutually exclusive classes (DupRoute routing artifacts, Institutional roads,
# and records with null/empty DOT_FCLASS that cannot be classified).
_status_ok  = ~gdf_centerlines["STATUS"].isin(["Retired", "Planned"])
_not_duprt  = gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("") != "DupRoute"
_fclass_ok  = (
    gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].notna()
    & ~gdf_centerlines["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Instituitional", ""])  # note: source-data typo
)
# Ramp/CD segments may have blank DOT_FCLASS but a valid DOT_RTNAME — keep them.
_is_ramp_seg = (
    gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("R", na=False)
    | gdf_centerlines["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("").str.contains("C", na=False)
) & _not_duprt

active_cl = gdf_centerlines[_status_ok & _not_duprt & (_fclass_ok | _is_ramp_seg)].copy()
print(f"Active centerlines: {len(active_cl):,} of {len(gdf_centerlines):,}")


Active centerlines: 25,268 of 25,358




B2 — Classify Segments
Three mutually exclusive boolean flags per segment. Primary authority is DOT_FCLASS for freeway identification; DOT_RTNAME (UDOT LRS) supplements with explicit route numbers and ramp/CD facility characters. ONEWAY in (1, 2) elevates divided Principal Arterials into the freeway class (domain CVDomain_OneWay: 0=Two-way, 1=One-way direction of arc, 2=One-way opposite direction of arc).
DOT_RTNAME layout (11 characters, zero-padded):
  [0:4]  Route number      e.g. "0015" = I-15, "0154" = Bangerter
  [4]    LRS direction     "P" = primary (NB/EB),  "N" = negative (SB/WB)
  [5]    Facility type     " " = mainline, "R" = ramp, "C" = collector-distributor
  [6:]   Sequence/suffix   varies
These flags flow into every endpoint pool and type-tier decision, making this the primary place to audit when snapping results look wrong.


Show the code
# ── Controlled-access corridors in the Wasatch Front study area ───────────
# Adding / removing a route here is the primary tuning lever for freeway coverage.
# These are the 4-character UDOT route numbers (DOT_RTNAME[0:4]).
FREEWAY_ROUTES = {
    "0015",   # Interstate 15         (primary N-S spine)
    "0067",   # Legacy Parkway        (limited-access, no ramps)
    "0080",   # Interstate 80         (E-W, SLC to Parley's Canyon)
    "0084",   # Interstate 84         (Ogden corridor)
    "0089",   # US-89                 (surface in places — watch for mis-classification)
    "0092",   # Timpanogos Highway    (SR-92, American Fork Canyon)
    "0154",   # Bangerter Highway     (semi-controlled ring road)
    "0177",   # West Davis Corridor   (new build, limited segments)
    "0201",   # SR-201                (West Valley connector)
    "0215",   # Interstate 215        (belt route, SLC)
}

rtname  = active_cl["DOT_RTNAME"].fillna("")

# ── Shared exclusion flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Ramp/CD character check: DOT_RTNAME LIKE '%R%' or '%C%', excluding DupRoute.
_has_R_or_C = rtname.str.contains("R", na=False) | rtname.str.contains("C", na=False)
_not_duprt  = rtname != "DupRoute"
_not_ramp_cd = ~_has_R_or_C | ~_not_duprt  # True when segment is NOT a ramp/CD

# ── Class 1: Freeway Mainline ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Primary: DOT_FCLASS identifies interstate / other freeway geometry.
# Secondary: explicit UDOT route number is a controlled-access corridor.
# Tertiary: Principal Arterial with ONEWAY in ('1','2') represents a
#           physically separated directional roadway.
#           ONEWAY domain (CVDomain_OneWay): 0=Two-way, 1=One-way direction
#           of arc, 2=One-way opposite direction of arc.
# All three require exclusion of ramp/CD facility chars and DupRoute aliases.
_is_fclass_fwy = active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway"])
_is_named_fwy  = rtname.str.slice(0, 4).isin(FREEWAY_ROUTES)
_is_dir_pa = (
    (active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"] == "Principal Arterial")
    & active_cl["ONEWAY"].isin(["1", "2"])
)

active_cl["is_freeway"] = (
    (_is_fclass_fwy | _is_named_fwy | _is_dir_pa)
    & ~_has_R_or_C
    & _not_duprt
)

# ── Class 2: Ramps and CDs ────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Strictly determined by DOT_RTNAME structure: any R or C facility character,
# excluding the DupRoute administrative alias.
active_cl["is_interchange"] = _has_R_or_C & _not_duprt

# ── Class 3: Surface Streets ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Everything that is not freeway mainline or ramp/CD.
# Explicitly excludes DOT_FCLASS values that fall outside the three classes
# (already filtered in B1, but guard here for completeness).
active_cl["is_surface"] = (
    ~active_cl["is_freeway"]
    & ~active_cl["is_interchange"]
    & active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].notna()
    & ~active_cl["DOT_FCLASS"].isin(["Interstate", "Other Freeway", "Instituitional", ""])  # note: source-data typo
)

print("Segment-level classification:")
print(f"  is_freeway     (mainline):   {active_cl['is_freeway'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_interchange (ramp / CD):  {active_cl['is_interchange'].sum():,}")
print(f"  is_surface     (all others): {active_cl['is_surface'].sum():,}")
print(f"  unclassified   (none):       {(~active_cl['is_freeway'] & ~active_cl['is_interchange'] & ~active_cl['is_surface']).sum():,}")
print(f"  both fwy + ic  (gore segs):  {(active_cl['is_freeway'] & active_cl['is_interchange']).sum():,}")
print(f"  total active:                {len(active_cl):,}")


Segment-level classification:
  is_freeway     (mainline):   2,182
  is_interchange (ramp / CD):  1,371
  is_surface     (all others): 21,715
  unclassified   (none):       0
  both fwy + ic  (gore segs):  0
  total active:                25,268




B3 — Extract Raw Endpoints
Extract the start and end point of every active segment into a flat GeoDataFrame (two rows per segment). Carries FULLNAME, DOT_RTNAME, and the three topology flags forward for direction resolution and deduplication downstream.


Show the code
# Explode MultiLineString → LineString so get_point works correctly
active_cl = active_cl.explode(index_parts=False).reset_index(drop=True)
print(
    f"After explode: {len(active_cl):,} segments (was {len(gdf_centerlines[~gdf_centerlines['STATUS'].isin(['Retired', 'Planned'])]):,})"
)

starts = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, 0)
ends = shapely.get_point(active_cl.geometry.values, -1)
print(f"Null starts after explode: {shapely.is_missing(starts).sum()}")  # should be 0

xs_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_x(starts), shapely.get_x(ends)])
ys_all = np.concatenate([shapely.get_y(starts), shapely.get_y(ends)])
tile = lambda col: np.concatenate([active_cl[col].values, active_cl[col].values])

gdf_ep_raw = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    {
        "geometry": np.concatenate([starts, ends]),
        "seg_idx": tile("index")
        if "index" in active_cl.columns
        else np.concatenate([active_cl.index.values, active_cl.index.values]),
        "x_round": np.round(xs_all, 2),
        "y_round": np.round(ys_all, 2),
        "fullname": tile("FULLNAME"),
        "dot_rtname": tile("DOT_RTNAME"),
        "is_freeway": tile("is_freeway"),
        "is_interchange": tile("is_interchange"),
        "is_surface": tile("is_surface"),
        "is_start": np.concatenate([np.ones(len(active_cl), bool), np.zeros(len(active_cl), bool)]),
    },
    crs=CRS_UTM,
).reset_index(drop=True)

print(f"Raw endpoint records: {len(gdf_ep_raw):,}  (2 × {len(active_cl):,} segments)")


After explode: 25,268 segments (was 25,338)
Null starts after explode: 0
Raw endpoint records: 50,536  (2 × 25,268 segments)




B4 — Deduplicate by Coordinate
Group raw endpoints by rounded XY (millimetre precision). Aggregate type flags with OR — a coordinate is is_freeway=True if any freeway segment terminates there.


Show the code
ep_agg = gdf_ep_raw.groupby(["x_round", "y_round"], as_index=False).agg(
    is_freeway=("is_freeway", "any"),
    is_interchange=("is_interchange", "any"),
    is_surface=("is_surface", "any"),
    fw_route_count=(
        "dot_rtname",
        lambda s: s[gdf_ep_raw.loc[s.index, "is_freeway"]].str[:4].nunique(),
    ),
)
ep_agg["geometry"] = gpd.points_from_xy(ep_agg["x_round"], ep_agg["y_round"])
gdf_ep_unique = gpd.GeoDataFrame(ep_agg, crs=CRS_UTM)

print(f"Unique endpoints: {len(gdf_ep_unique):,}")
print(
    f"  freeway-only   : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  freeway + ramp : {(gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange']).sum():,}"
)
print(
    f"  ramp-only      : {(~gdf_ep_unique['is_freeway'] & gdf_ep_unique['is_interchange'] & ~gdf_ep_unique['is_surface']).sum():,}"
)
print(f"  surface        : {gdf_ep_unique['is_surface'].sum():,}")


Unique endpoints: 23,026
  freeway-only   : 1,557
  freeway + ramp : 671
  ramp-only      : 683
  surface        : 20,406




B5 — Assign Endpoint Directions
assign_endpoint_directions calls _resolve_direction per unique coordinate, aggregating direction from all contributing raw segments with freeway-first priority.


Show the code
gdf_ep_unique = assign_endpoint_directions(gdf_ep_unique, gdf_ep_raw)

print(f"Endpoints with direction: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] != '').sum():,}")
print(f"Endpoints direction-agnostic: {(gdf_ep_unique['ep_allowed_dirs'] == '').sum():,}")


Endpoints with direction: 3,227
Endpoints direction-agnostic: 19,799




B6 — Assign Endpoint Type
Six mutually-exclusive ep_type labels derived from the three boolean flags, mirroring the node_type cascade so type-tier matching compares like with like. gore is checked first — a point where freeway and ramp both terminate is always a gore regardless of surface contact.


Show the code
fw = gdf_ep_unique["is_freeway"]
ic = gdf_ep_unique["is_interchange"]
sf = gdf_ep_unique["is_surface"]

gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] = np.select(
    [
        fw &  ic,            # freeway/ramp junction
        fw &  sf,            # at-grade freeway/surface crossing or frontage
        fw & ~ic & ~sf,      # pure freeway mainline endpoint
        ~fw & ic &  sf,      # ramp-to-arterial terminal
        ~fw & ic & ~sf,      # mid-ramp endpoint
    ],
    ["gore", "fwy_sf", "fwy", "ramp_sf", "ramp"],
    default="surface",
)

print("Endpoint type distribution:")
print(gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].value_counts().rename("count").to_frame().to_string())

title: Node Classification
section: 5 Part C — Interchange Node Direction Enrichment
text:
5 Part C — Interchange Node Direction Enrichment
For interchange-only nodes (pass c), the directional signal is inherited one hop from adjacent freeway mainline links. This enrichment is done explicitly here (not hidden inside snap_nodes) so the lookup table is inspectable.


Show the code
# Build a flat edge list: (node_N, FT, DIRECTION) — vectorised via pd.concat
edges = pd.concat([
    gdf_links[["A", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"A": "N"}),
    gdf_links[["B", "FT_2027", "DIRECTION"]].rename(columns={"B": "N"}),
], ignore_index=True)
edges["is_freeway_link"] = edges["FT_2027"].isin(FW_CODES)
edges["DIRECTION"]       = edges["DIRECTION"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper()

CARDINAL = {"NB", "SB", "EB", "WB"}

# For each interchange-only node, collect cardinals from adjacent freeway links.
ic_only_mask = ~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node
ic_only_Ns   = set(gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "N"])

adj_fw = (
    edges[edges["N"].isin(ic_only_Ns) & edges["is_freeway_link"] & edges["DIRECTION"].isin(CARDINAL)]
    .groupby("N")["DIRECTION"]
    .apply(lambda s: ",".join(sorted(set(s))))
)

gdf_nodes["ic_fw_directions"] = gdf_nodes["N"].map(adj_fw).fillna("")

resolved = (gdf_nodes.loc[ic_only_mask, "ic_fw_directions"] != "").sum()
total_ic  = ic_only_mask.sum()
print(f"Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: {resolved:,} of {total_ic:,}")
print(f"  remaining {total_ic - resolved:,} are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link)")


Interchange-only nodes with freeway-neighbor direction: 6 of 509
  remaining 503 are direction-agnostic (no adjacent freeway link)

title: Node Classification
text:
6 Part D — Node Snapping
Hierarchical execution — first-call-wins. Nodes snapped in an earlier pass are skipped by subsequent passes.

D1 — Freeway and Interchange Snapping
Four passes in topology-priority order: (a) pure freeway, (a2) at-grade freeway crossings, (b) gore/gore_sf, (c) mid-ramp. All include fwy_sf in the D1 debug export so at-grade crossing snaps can be verified before running surface snapping.


Show the code
# ── Endpoint pools ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# Pass (a) — fwy nodes → fwy endpoints only.
#   Strict pool: no ramp or surface endpoints. Prevents divided-highway collapse
#   where NB and SB nodes both compete for the same non-directional endpoint.
ep_fwy_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"].copy()

# Pass (a2) — fwy_sf nodes → fwy_sf + fwy + gore endpoints.
#   fwy_sf nodes touch freeway links, so the freeway direction signal is reliable.
#   Surface endpoints are intentionally excluded from this pool — if no fwy-class
#   endpoint is within range the node falls through to D2 (surface pass) where
#   surface endpoints are available as a Tier-1 fallback.
ep_fwy_sf = (
    pd.concat(
        [
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy_sf"],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy"],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"],
        ]
    )
    .drop_duplicates()
    .copy()
)

# Pass (b) — gore + gore_sf nodes → gore endpoint pool.
#   Core pool: gore eps and multi-route fwy eps (major interchange hubs).
#   Extended with ramp_sf for gore_sf nodes (all three classes meet at grade).
#   _TYPE_TIER rejects ramp_sf for plain gore nodes, so the extension is harmless.
ep_gore = (
    pd.concat(
        [
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "gore"],
            gdf_ep_unique[
                (gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "fwy") & (gdf_ep_unique["fw_route_count"] >= 2)
            ],
            gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"] == "ramp_sf"],  # for gore_sf nodes
        ]
    )
    .drop_duplicates()
    .copy()
)

# Pass (c) — ramp nodes → ramp + ramp_sf endpoints.
#   ramp_sf nodes are excluded: they have surface links and route to D2 where
#   their at-grade topology is Tier-0.
ep_ic_only = gdf_ep_unique[gdf_ep_unique["ep_type"].isin(["ramp", "ramp_sf"])].copy()

print(f"Pass (a)  pool — fwy:                  {len(ep_fwy_only):,}")
print(f"Pass (a2) pool — fwy_sf + fwy + gore:  {len(ep_fwy_sf):,}")
print(f"Pass (b)  pool — gore + ramp_sf:       {len(ep_gore):,}")
print(f"Pass (c)  pool — ramp + ramp_sf:       {len(ep_ic_only):,}")

# ── Node masks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# gore_sf (fwy+ramp+surface): included in pass (b) alongside gore nodes;
#   _TYPE_TIER gives it access to ramp_sf endpoints at Tier-1.
# ramp_sf (ramp+surface): skips pass (c), routes directly to D2 surface pass.
print()
print(f"Pass (a)  nodes — fwy:     {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (a2) nodes — fwy_sf:  {(is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node &  is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")
print(f"Pass (b)  nodes — gore:    {(is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node).sum():,}")

gdf_nodes_snapped = gdf_nodes.copy()

# _claimed: grows across passes — prevents two nodes in different passes from
# landing on the same physical endpoint.  The Gale-Shapley algorithm ensures
# uniqueness within a single pass; this set enforces it across passes whose
# endpoint pools overlap (e.g. gore endpoints appear in both ep_gore and
# ep_surface).
_claimed: frozenset = frozenset()

# Pass (a) — fwy nodes → fwy-only endpoint pool
#   Mask: fwy+ramp+surface combinations excluded — they go to passes (a2), (b), or D2.
#   Type tier: fwy ep = Tier-0 | gore/fwy_sf ep = Tier-1.
#   Direction: link_directions (freeway-priority cascade).
#   Threshold: 500m (model centroids can sit well off the physical carriageway).
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_fwy_only,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Only",
    direction_col="link_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (a2) — fwy_sf nodes (fwy+surface, no ramp) → fwy_sf endpoint pool
#   At-grade freeway crossings: the node touches a freeway AND a surface street.
#   Type tier: fwy_sf ep = Tier-0 | fwy/gore ep = Tier-1.
#   Surface endpoints are NOT in this pool — nodes that miss here fall to D2 where
#   surface endpoints are available as a graceful fallback.
#   Direction: link_directions (freeway component provides the cardinal signal).
#   Threshold: 500m.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_fwy_sf,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & ~is_ramp_node & is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Surface",
    direction_col="link_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (b) — gore + gore_sf nodes → gore endpoint pool
#   gore:    fwy+ramp, no surface — classic elevated junction.
#            Type tier: gore ep = Tier-0 | fwy/ramp/fwy_sf ep = Tier-1.
#   gore_sf: fwy+ramp+surface — at-grade interchange.
#            Type tier: gore ep = Tier-0 | fwy_sf/ramp_sf/fwy/ramp ep = Tier-1.
#            ramp_sf eps are in the pool for gore_sf; _TYPE_TIER rejects them
#            for plain gore nodes, so no false matches on elevated junctions.
#   Direction: fw_directions (freeway-only majority-vote, isolates mainline
#              direction from the ramp/CD signal at the same node).
#   Threshold: 500m.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_gore,
    node_mask=is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=500, label="Freeway_Gore",
    direction_col="fw_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Pass (c) — ramp nodes (mid-ramp only, no surface) → ramp endpoint pool
#   Direction: ic_fw_directions (inherited one hop from adjacent freeway — see Part C).
#   ramp nodes have no surface links so link_directions is unreliable;
#   freeway-neighbor direction is the best available signal.
#   Threshold: 100m (mid-ramp nodes sit close to physical endpoints).
#   fwy_sf and ramp_sf nodes are intentionally excluded — both have surface links
#   and route to the surface pass (D2) where their at-grade topology is Tier-0.
gdf_nodes_snapped, _claimed = snap_nodes(
    gdf_nodes_snapped, ep_ic_only,
    node_mask=~is_fwy_node & is_ramp_node & ~is_surface_node & ~is_transit_node,
    max_distance_m=100, label="Interchange_Only",
    direction_col="ic_fw_directions", target_id_cols=["x_round", "y_round"],
    excluded_ep_coords=_claimed,
)

# Merge co-located platforms (same stop_name) to a single centroid.
# geometry column is plain shapely objects after the lambda — no inherited CRS.
# gdf_rail_stops is already in CRS_UTM (reprojected at import), so set directly.
rail_stops_merged = (
    gdf_rail_stops.groupby("stop_name")
    .apply(
        lambda g: pd.Series(
            {
                "stop_id": ",".join(g["stop_id"].astype(str).unique()),
                "geometry": g.geometry.union_all().centroid,
            }
        ),
        include_groups=False,
    )
    .reset_index()
)
gdf_rail_stops_grouped = gpd.GeoDataFrame(
    rail_stops_merged,
    geometry="geometry",
    crs=CRS_UTM,
)

CC Attachment Nodes
Centroid connector attachment nodes are the network-side endpoints of FT_2027 = 1 (centroid connector) links — the taps where zone centroids connect to the road network. Zone centroids themselves (N < 3600) are excluded; only the network attachment nodes (N ≥ 3600) are retained.
These nodes are snapped to the nearest physical centerline vertex (not segment endpoint) using a simple STRtree nearest-vertex lookup — no direction filtering, no greedy assignment. The result is written as a second layer in nodes_snapped.gpkg so Stage 2 can use them as additional centerline split points.

title: Transfer Model Attributes to True-Shape Links
text:
4 Part B — Build Piece Graph
Build a networkx Graph from the physical link pieces. Each node is labelled real_junction, pseudonode, or internal based on whether it coincides with a snapped model node and whether that node is a pseudonode.


Show the code
print("Building piece graph …")
piece_graph = build_piece_graph(gdf_pieces, gdf_nodes_snapped)

n_nodes = piece_graph.number_of_nodes()
n_edges = piece_graph.number_of_edges()
type_counts = {}
for _, attrs in piece_graph.nodes(data=True):
    t = attrs.get("node_type", "internal")
    type_counts[t] = type_counts.get(t, 0) + 1

print(f"Piece graph: {n_nodes:,} nodes | {n_edges:,} edges")
print(f"Node types:")
for t, cnt in sorted(type_counts.items()):
    print(f"  {t:20s}: {cnt:,}")

title: Transfer Model Attributes to True-Shape Links
text:
5 Part C — Assemble True-Shape Chains
For each dissolved link A→B, perform a constrained BFS through the piece graph from A’s snap coordinate to B’s snap coordinate. The BFS cannot traverse through real_junction nodes other than B — this ensures the path stays within the physical bounds of a single dissolved link.


Show the code
print(f"Assembling {len(gdf_dissolved):,} dissolved links …")

trueshape_geometries: list = []
trueshape_methods: list[str] = []
n_unresolved = 0
n_no_path = 0

for i, (_, row) in enumerate(gdf_dissolved.iterrows()):
    if i > 0 and i % 2000 == 0:
        print(f"  {i:,} / {len(gdf_dissolved):,} …")

    a_n, b_n = int(row["A"]), int(row["B"])
    a_coord = node_coord_lookup.get(a_n)
    b_coord = node_coord_lookup.get(b_n)

    if a_coord is None or b_coord is None:
        n_unresolved += 1
        a_pt = node_geom_lookup.get(a_n)
        b_pt = node_geom_lookup.get(b_n)
        fallback = LineString([a_pt, b_pt]) if (a_pt and b_pt) else None
        trueshape_geometries.append(fallback)
        trueshape_methods.append("unresolved_no_snap")
        continue

    pieces = assemble_chain(piece_graph, a_coord, b_coord)

    if pieces is None:
        n_no_path += 1
        a_pt = node_geom_lookup.get(a_n)
        b_pt = node_geom_lookup.get(b_n)
        fallback = LineString([a_pt, b_pt]) if (a_pt and b_pt) else None
        trueshape_geometries.append(fallback)
        trueshape_methods.append("unresolved_no_path")
    else:
        merged = linemerge(pieces) if len(pieces) > 1 else (pieces[0] if pieces else None)
        trueshape_geometries.append(merged)
        trueshape_methods.append("dissolved")

n_dissolved = trueshape_methods.count("dissolved")
print(f"\nDissolved (true-shape):  {n_dissolved:,} ({n_dissolved/len(gdf_dissolved):.1%})")
print(f"Unresolved (no snap):    {n_unresolved:,}")
print(f"Unresolved (no path):    {n_no_path:,}")
